creator: Alexander Terp
description:
A function that calls itself infinitely runs forever with no stack overflow detection or recursion depth limit.

**Repro:**
```
fn infinite():
    return infinite()

infinite()  // Hangs forever, must kill process
```

Most languages impose a recursion limit (e.g. Python's default 1000). Without one, accidental infinite recursion requires the user to manually kill the process.

**Test files:** `bugs/functions/t124_fn_recursive_depth_limit.rad`
title: Infinite recursion has no depth limit - must be killed externally
